message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "high enchantment (>=5) sword adds 30% surcharge"
Passing tests so far: 11

Test: sword enchantment 5, not cursed. Expected 145. Math: 100 base + 30 high-ench (30%) + 10 first = 140, + 5 fee = 145.

Add high-enchantment surcharge: if item.enchantment >= 5, add 30% of base premium (independent of base, like cursed). Apply only to non-component items.

Run `pnpm test`. Working dir: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and add the minimal code needed to make the failing test pass.
Now I'll add the minimal high-enchantment surcharge logic.

input.old_string: type Item = { type: string; cursed?: boolean };
input.new_string: type Item = { type: string; cursed?: boolean; enchantment?: number };

content.text:
All 12 tests pass (11 previous + 1 new).

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added `enchantment?: number` to `Item` type and a `highEnchSurcharge` calculation (30% of base premium when `enchantment >= 5`) to the non-component premium calculation. Mirrors the existing `curseSurcharge` pattern.
- **Result**: All 12 tests pass (24 still as `it.todo`).
- **Approach**: Minimal additive change — applies only to non-components (the cursed surcharge path), independent of base like the cursed surcharge, gated by `enchantment >= 5`.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.ts`
